item.title: Ex-Bucknell University coach charged in death of freshman football player
item.description: A former Bucknell University coach has been charged in the death of a freshman football player who collapsed during practice and died in 2024.

item.title: Mom who said her toddlers died after vaccinations charged with murder
item.description: A grand jury indicted Andrea Shaw, who is accused of suffocating her 18-month-old twins, on two counts of first-degree murder.

item.title: NATO summit in Turkey begins as Trump pushes for more defense spending
item.description: Amid simmering tensions between the U.S. and some NATO allies over Iran and Greenland, President Trump will continue to press other countries to boost defense spending.

item.title: Tanker struck as Iran threatens to abandon talks over Trump's threats
item.description: A tanker was struck by a projectile in the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. and Iran continued exchanging threats with peace talks on hold.

item.title: Toyota, in $3.6 billion move, shifting Tacoma production to Texas from Mexico
item.description: Toyota says it's shifting most production of its mid-size Tacoma pickup truck from Mexico to the United States as part of a $3.6 billion investment in its San Antonio, Texas, plant.

item.title: Democrats Stevens and El-Sayed face off in primary debate for Michigan Senate
item.description: The debate comes days after a third candidate, Mallory McMorrow, suspended her campaign.

item.title: Senate Democrats push Trump-affiliated companies for answers about IRS settlement
item.description: Democratic Sens. Elizabeth Warren, Chuck Schumer and Ron Wyden are probing whether companies affiliated with the Trump family are included in a DOJ deal to resolve a lawsuit brought by President Trump.

item.title: French President Macron safe after bomb blasts near hotel in Damascus
item.description: France's government says President Emmanuel Macron is safe and will continue his visit to Syria after two bombs exploded outside his hotel in Damascus.

item.title: Trump says U.S. will win war "one way or the other" as Iran holds Khamenei's funeral
item.description: The U.S. is "going to win one way or the other," President Trump said Monday, as Iran held a colossal public funeral for its slain supreme leader.

item.title: FIFA criticized for lifting U.S. star's red card suspension after Trump phone call
item.description: Europe's soccer governing body and prominent commentators have criticized the decision, which allows Folarin Balogun to play against Belgium.

item.title: Belgium knocks out U.S. men 4-1, ending the Americans' World Cup run in Seattle
item.description: Belgium defeated the U.S. Men's National Team​ 4-1 on Monday night in the World Cup round of 16 knockout match in Seattle, ending the Americans' hopes of reaching the quarterfinals for the first time in 24 years.

item.title: See the full U.S. men's soccer schedule for the 2026 World Cup
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team kicked off its 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign against Paraguay at SoFi Stadium on Friday.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Top Democrats call on Platner to drop out as sexual assault allegation emerges
item.description: A Maine woman told Politico that Graham Platner entered her home in late 2021 and forced himself on her. Platner denies the allegation.

item.title: Trump heading to NATO summit amid tensions over defense spending, Iran war
item.description: President Trump will leave for Turkey on Monday night to attend the annual NATO summit. The president is set to meet with allies he has chastised for insufficient support in the war in Iran and defense spending. Leo Shane III, Politico defense reporter, joins CBS News to discuss what to expect.

item.title: Dayslong preliminary hearing for Charlie Kirk's alleged killer is underway
item.description: Prosecutors will present their case this week against Tyler Robinson, the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk.

item.title: Suspect in Charlie Kirk killing appears in court
item.description: Tyler Robinson, the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk, was in court Monday for a critical hearing. Carter Evans reports.

item.title: Chris Richards on red card controversy, U.S.-Belgium match: "Business as usual"
item.description: U.S. Men's National Team defender Chris Richards spoke with "CBS Evening News" anchor Tony Dokoupil in an interview ahead of the knockout match against Belgium.

item.title: USMNT defender Chris Richards reacts to Folarin Balogun's red card suspension being lifted
item.description: USMNT defender Chris Richards speaks with Tony Dokoupil about the controversial decision to lift the suspension against Folarin Balogun and his team's ongoing run through the World Cup.

item.title: 2 National Guard members on patrol in Memphis fatally shoot man, police say
item.description: Two Tennessee National Guard members fatally shot a man in Memphis who turned toward them with a gun during a downtown pursuit, authorities said.

item.title: U.S. says China's nuclear weapons buildup "of great concern" after missile test
item.description: China's military test-launched a long-range ballistic missile from a nuclear-powered submarine in the South Pacific, drawing protest and concern from countries in the region.

item.title: Balogun's red card suspension lifted after Trump called FIFA chief
item.description: Balogun's reinstatement came after President Trump called FIFA President Gianni Infantino last week and asked for a review of the red card suspension.

item.title: Trump says he saw play that led to red card and asked FIFA president for review
item.description: President Trump said he spoke to FIFA President Gianni Infantino after seeing the play and asked for a review.

item.title: 7/6: CBS Evening News
item.description: New Jersey hit with powerful storms after heat wave; passenger recounts seaplane's hard landing in New York City's East River.

item.title: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev breaks down how Trump Accounts work
item.description: President Trump rang the New York Stock Exchange opening bell from the Oval Office on Monday to celebrate the launch of the new investment account program for children dubbed "Trump Accounts." Robinhood chairman and CEO Vlad Tenev, who assisted with the project, joins to discuss.

item.title: 7/6: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Democrats withdrawing Platner endorsements after sexual assault allegation; Russian attack on Kyiv looms over upcoming NATO summit.

item.title: Children who died on Wisconsin's Geneva Lake found in sunken boat, police say
item.description: Three children who died when a boat capsized on Geneva Lake in Wisconsin amid a sudden storm last week were found inside the sunken vessel, police said.

item.title: AI actor Tilly Norwood set to star in first feature film
item.description: AI-generated actor Tilly Norwood is set to star in her first feature film, with her creator saying that "art will be imitating life."

item.title: U.K. fighter jets intercept Russian plane near aircraft carrier off Iceland
item.description: The Russian Bear-F plane "repeatedly approached" the HMS Prince of Wales aircraft carrier, flying "unnecessarily close" and dropping sonar devices, according to the U.K.'s defense ministry.

item.title: Phil Weiser projected to win Democratic primary for Colorado governor
item.description: Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser is the winner in the pivotal race to be the Democratic nominee for governor in Colorado, AP projects.
